title: Project Proposal
text: This project explores FinTech adoption and user engagement using the COFINFAD dataset, with the aim of generating data-driven insights into digital financial service usage. With the rapid growth of FinTech, digital financial services have become an increasingly important part of modern financial activity. However, adoption is unlikely to be uniform across all user groups, as differences in demographics, behavior, and engagement may shape how individuals interact with these services. In this context, the COFINFAD dataset provides a valuable opportunity to examine user characteristics, transaction patterns, product adoption, application usage, and customer satisfaction from a data-driven perspective. This project aims to better understand which groups are more likely to adopt FinTech services, what factors may be associated with adoption, and which users may remain less engaged. Ultimately, the project seeks to generate insights that can support broader FinTech adoption strategies, improve customer understanding, and contribute to financial inclusion.

title: Project Proposal
text:
Problem Statement
Although FinTech has grown rapidly and become more integrated into everyday financial activity, user adoption and engagement are unlikely to be consistent across all customer groups. Differences in demographic background, financial behavior, product usage, and digital engagement may lead to distinct patterns in how customers interact with FinTech services. However, these patterns are often not immediately visible without systematic analysis.
In addition, customer activity may change over time, making it important to understand not only current usage behavior but also temporal trends and potential future developments. At the same time, customers may form meaningful segments with different levels of engagement, adoption, and satisfaction, while some observed differences across groups may require statistical validation rather than simple visual inspection.
Therefore, there is a need for a data-driven analytical approach that can examine user behavior from multiple perspectives. This project addresses this gap by using the COFINFAD dataset to explore temporal patterns, identify customer segments, and validate key relationships and differences across user groups, with the aim of generating deeper insights into FinTech adoption and engagement.
